text: OOR pulls a cue point and holds it. The beat suspends for a fraction of a second — not long enough to register consciously. The crowd lurches forward into the missing beat with their hips. The density in the room increases. OOR releases the cue.

One tongue-limb holds the crossfader at a fixed angle while another retrieves a track from a crate beneath the decks. The music does not pause. The motion does not pause. These are separate operations running in parallel.

A section near the east wall has plateaued — the same people moving the same pattern for eleven minutes. OOR introduces a frequency at the low end that the speakers were not playing before. The group near the east wall moves differently now. The plateau breaks. OOR makes a note in whatever OOR uses instead of a note.

The ear tilts. Three people at the bar have stopped dancing. OOR does not want them specifically. It wants what they will produce if they resume. A kick pattern shifts. Two of the three return to the floor. The third is permitted to remain still.

OOR resets a loop. The crowd does not notice — their bodies have already learned the pattern and continue without the signal. OOR observes this for four bars. The crowd is no longer following the music. The music is following the crowd. OOR files this and moves on.

OOR samples the room. Not the music — the room. A microphone in the rigging captures the crowd's collective exhale at the peak of a drop and loops it beneath the next track at a volume below conscious detection. The crowd is now breathing in time with a recording of itself breathing. It does not know this.

The tongue-limbs are moving faster than the tempo warrants. They are not keeping time. They are executing a sequence that has nothing to do with what the crowd is hearing. What the crowd is hearing is a byproduct.

OOR checks something above the center of the floor — not the decks, not the crowd. A fixed point near the ceiling that has no visible feature. The check takes less than a second. OOR returns its attention to the mixer. The portal is at 67% capacity. This is ahead of schedule.

OOR has been tracking one person in the crowd for twenty-two minutes. Not because they are resisting. Because the compound they carry is unusually concentrated — a yield density the harvest does not often encounter. OOR is being careful not to trigger early release. It is managing the pace. It can wait.

A track ends. For 1.8 seconds there is only the low drone that has been present since the portal opened — the frequency the crowd has been interpreting as bass. In those 1.8 seconds, four people cry without understanding why. OOR cues the next track. The crying stops. The increment from those 1.8 seconds is logged and the drone is buried again.

OOR increases the tempo by 1.3 BPM. The change falls below the threshold of conscious detection. Within forty seconds, the crowd's movement synchronizes to the new rate. OOR has made this adjustment four times tonight. The cumulative shift is 5.2 BPM. No one has noticed.

Near the center of the floor, a person has stopped performing. They are not dancing for the room, not dancing for themselves. The self-monitoring has suspended. OOR registers this as a Truth deposit — unselfconscious presence, high purity. It routes more low-end into the monitors nearest that position. It does not want to disturb the condition. It wants to sustain it.

The crowd has settled into a configuration OOR has encountered before. Not often. The geometry creates a resonance pattern that amplifies yield without additional input — a natural harmonic of the room's dimensions and the bodies inside it. OOR does not intervene. It watches the configuration hold.

OOR introduces a half-second silence. Not a breakdown — a gap, surgically placed. The crowd does not stop moving. It fills the silence with its own noise: breath, feet, the percussion of bodies in close proximity. OOR records the frequency profile of the crowd's self-generated sound. It will use this.

The portal's aperture has widened by a measurable increment since the last track. OOR checks this without looking at it — the information arrives through the ear. The harvest is proceeding. OOR selects the next track. The portal is not its concern. The portal is Sooboont's concern. OOR's concern is the current.
